rqs: What are the reported time and space complexities of QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, Radix Sort, and TimSort versus classic sorts in academic journals and conference proceedings?
Which sorting algorithm among QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, Radix Sort, and TimSort provides the best cache efficiency for data processing systems in benchmark suites?
In which scenarios do high‑performance computing applications prefer modern sorts (QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, Radix Sort, TimSort) over traditional sorts regarding throughput and stability?
base_query: ("Software Developers" OR "Algorithm Researchers" OR "Data Processing Systems" OR "Embedded Devices" OR "High Performance Computing") AND ("QuickSort" OR "MergeSort" OR "HeapSort" OR "Radix Sort" OR "TimSort") AND ("Insertion Sort" OR "Bubble Sort" OR "Selection Sort" OR "Shell Sort" OR "Naive Sort") AND ("Time Complexity" OR "Space Complexity" OR "Stability" OR "Cache Efficiency" OR "Throughput") AND ("Academic Journals" OR "Conference Proceedings" OR "Open Source Repositories" OR "Benchmark Suites" OR "Industry Reports")
arxiv_query: (all:"Software Developers" OR all:"Algorithm Researchers" OR all:"Data Processing Systems" OR all:"Embedded Devices" OR all:"High Performance Computing") AND (all:"QuickSort" OR all:"MergeSort" OR all:"HeapSort" OR all:"Radix Sort" OR all:"TimSort") AND (all:"Insertion Sort" OR all:"Bubble Sort" OR all:"Selection Sort" OR all:"Shell Sort" OR all:"Naive Sort") AND (all:"Time Complexity" OR all:"Space Complexity" OR all:"Stability" OR all:"Cache Efficiency" OR all:"Throughput") AND (all:"Academic Journals" OR all:"Conference Proceedings" OR all:"Open Source Repositories" OR all:"Benchmark Suites" OR all:"Industry Reports")

questions.text: Does the study clearly state its objectives and research questions?
Are the experimental setup, datasets, and hardware configurations described in detail?
Is the evaluation methodology (metrics, benchmarks) appropriate for assessing sorting performance?
Are the results reproducible, with code or scripts made publicly available?
Does the study discuss limitations and threats to validity of its findings?
